headline: AI hardware trades stalling - optical and memory stocks falling
detail: The AI hardware momentum trade is losing steam, with optical and memory names selling off. This is a direct sector headwind for AVGO and SMH, your two biggest chip exposures. If the rotation out of hardware accelerates, your largest losing position gets worse.

headline: Inflation above 4%, Fed's Warsh holds hawkish line
detail: Inflation printing over 4% with a hawkish Fed chair means rates stay higher for longer. High-multiple tech and AI names like PLTR and CRWD are the most vulnerable in this environment. On margin, elevated rates also increase your cost of carry on every position.

headline: OpenAI IPO reportedly delayed
detail: OpenAI pushing back its IPO cools some near-term AI sentiment and hype that has been propping up AI-adjacent names. This matters indirectly for PLTR and CRWD, which trade heavily on AI narrative momentum. Less IPO buzz can mean less retail enthusiasm for the whole sector.

headline: Trump says Iran violated ceasefire with Strait of Hormuz drone attacks
detail: Geopolitical flare-up in a critical shipping lane adds macro risk and uncertainty to markets. While not a direct tech catalyst, escalation spikes volatility and risk-off sentiment which hits leveraged positions hardest. Your margin book and SPAL in particular have no cushion for a surprise risk-off move.
